description_long: This is a partially constructed two-story house with a steeply pitched red gable roof and exposed brick walls. The structure features a large covered porch area supported by white columns, and several open sections reveal unfinished interiors. Windows are arranged irregularly, with some lacking frames or glass, and there is a prominent dormer on the main roof. The house sits on a rectangular plot with a visible foundation and some surrounding greenery. The roof overhangs provide shelter to the porch and entry areas, and the overall design suggests a blend of traditional and modern residential styles. The building appears to be in the mid-stages of construction, with some exterior elements incomplete.
description: A two-story, partially constructed house with a steep red gable roof, exposed brick walls, and a large covered porch supported by columns. The structure features irregular window placement and unfinished sections.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main entry area, large porch with columns, and exposed brickwork under the steep gable roof.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the open, unfinished sections of the house and the extended porch area.
description_view_2: Back view dominated by the expansive red roof and dormer, with minimal window visibility and a clear view of the foundation.
description_view_3: Rear-right view showing the side entry, multiple windows, and the unfinished balcony area.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with steps, covered porch supported by columns, and a simple overhang.
